_meta.description: Czech Republic steel market context — volumes only (kt). For dashboard integration.

methodology.production: WorldSteel / Czech Steel Union (Ocelářská unie) annual crude steel output
methodology.trade_third_countries: Eurostat DS-045409 HS72 — rolled products from/to non-EU countries only (GMK Center analysis)
methodology.trade_total: Czech Steel Union total — HS72/73 steel and steel products, ALL trading partners (EU + third countries). Large volumes explained by CZ re-processing trade: downstream plants (ŽDB, VTC, Velký Šenov, Veselí) import semi-finished/coils, process them, re-export as wire/tubes/strips.
methodology.apparent_consumption: Czech Steel Union methodology: production + total imports − total exports (HS72/73 steel products only — NOT manufactured goods). High absolute figure (~8 Mt in 2021) reflects CZ role as regional steel processing hub with significant re-export flows.
methodology.by_product: Czech Steel Union (2021 direct data); other years estimated from plant-level sources (Trinecke + Liberty press releases)

confidence_notes.high: production totals, Trinecke plant-level data, 2021 trade totals, 2023 third-country trade
confidence_notes.medium: apparent consumption totals, product category splits for 2020/2022/2023
confidence_notes.low: apparent consumption 2020/2022, plant-level product splits for Liberty 2022-2023

sources.name: Czech Steel Union (Ocelářská unie) via SteelOrbis — 2021 annual report
GMK Center — Czech steel consumption analysis, Feb 2026

production_crude_kt._note: Total Czech Republic crude steel production (kt). Source: Czech Steel Union / WorldSteel.

2023.note: WorldSteel: -21.1% yoy, due to Liberty Ostrava shutdown

production_by_category_kt._note: 2021 is from Czech Steel Union (direct). Other years estimated from plant-level data.

2020.note: Est: Liberty flat(~430) + Vitkovice(~150) + Trinecke wide(~270)

2021.note: Czech Steel Union direct; Liberty flat=680 kt

2022.note: Est: Liberty cut flat production amid energy crisis

2023.note: Est: Liberty ~100 kt + Vitkovice ~150 kt + Trinecke wide ~100 kt

2024.note: Est: Vitkovice plate ~150 + Trinecke wide ~50; Liberty=0

2020.note: Est: Trinecke + Liberty(pre-2021 level ~100) + VTC + ZV

2021.note: Czech Steel Union direct; Liberty tubes=136 kt

2023.note: Est: Liberty tube mill reduced; Trinecke + VTC + Veseli operational

2024.note: Est: Trinecke seamless + VTC + Veseli; Liberty=0

trade_total_kt._note: TOTAL trade (all countries incl EU + steel in manufactured goods). Czech Steel Union methodology (steel equivalent). Only 2021 is directly sourced; others estimated.

2020.note: Est based on 2021 peak and declining trend

trade_third_countries_rolled_kt._note: Only non-EU (third country) trade in rolled steel products (HS72). Source: Eurostat via GMK Center. Much smaller than total trade (EU intra-trade not included here).

2022.note: Est: 2023 long imports were 112 kt (-25% yoy → 2022=~150)
Est: 2023 flat imports were 126 kt (+13% yoy → 2022=~112)

2023.note: Eurostat/GMK: +13% yoy; HRC dominant at 62 kt
Eurostat/GMK: rebar 30 kt, bars 32 kt, wire 27 kt

apparent_consumption_kt._note: Czech Steel Union methodology: production + total imports − total exports. All figures in HS72/73 (steel products only — not manufactured goods). High values explained by CZ re-processing trade: downstream mills import coils/semis, process, re-export.

2022.note: Est: Prod 4200 + Imp ~7500 − Exp ~4600; EU demand slump

2023.note: Est: annualized from Q2 2023 data (1.5 Mt/Q × 4 ≈ 6000); GMK implies ~6 Mt

2024.note: GMK Center Feb 2026: 'market capacity is 5.6 Mt'; WorldSteel per-capita=532 kg × 10.9M pop
GMK Center / TŽ press release June 2025

rails.note: Only rail producer in CZ; heavy export

seamless_tubes.note: Produced but volumes not publicly split

share_of_cz_long_pct._note: TŽ long production / CZ total long production

nova_hut_ostrava.name: Nová Huť (Liberty Ostrava → SPV NH Ostrava from Jul 2025)

2022.note: Est: CZ total 4200 − Trinecke 2500 = 1700 (Liberty had energy/demand issues)

2024.note: Essentially zero (bankrupt, BF shut Oct 2023, full stop Dec 2023)

products_2021_kt._note: Liberty Ostrava 2021 press release (highest production since 2016)

share_of_cz_flat_pct._note: Liberty flat production / CZ total flat production

estimated_production_kt._note: Production not publicly disclosed; estimates based on capacity utilization signals

share_of_cz_flat_pct._note: Vitkovice flat / CZ total flat; 2024 high because Liberty=0

market_share_cz_production_pct._description: Plant share of total Czech crude steel production (kt / kt × 100). For Liberty: 0% in 2024 due to shutdown.

market_share_apparent_consumption_pct._description: Plant crude steel production / CZ apparent consumption × 100. Shows domestic supply coverage. NOTE: CZ is a net steel exporter — plants export most output, so this ratio > 100% is possible and normal.

trinecke_zelezarny._note: Even at 43% of apparent consumption, TŽ exports 69% of production → domestic supply from TŽ ≈ ~750 kt

key_market_insights: Czech Republic is a net steel IMPORTER in volume terms (imports 8.3 Mt vs production 4.7 Mt in 2021), but a major RE-EXPORTER: downstream mills import coils/semis/wire rod, process them, re-export as wire/tubes/strips/coated products
Apparent consumption peaked at ~8.1 Mt in 2021 (post-COVID rebound) and declined to ~5.6 Mt in 2024 — a 31% drop in 4 years
The decline is driven by: (1) Liberty Ostrava collapse 2023-2024, (2) EU steel demand recession, (3) energy cost crisis
In 2024, Třinecké železárny became effectively the only primary steel producer in CZ (~99% of crude steel)
Czech Republic ranks ~40th globally in steel production (was ~30th in 2021)
Steel consumption per capita 2024: 532 kg/person — still one of the highest in EU, driven by auto industry (Skoda, Toyota, Hyundai)
New owners (SPV NH Ostrava) took over Liberty in July 2025 — production restart expected; if successful, CZ output could recover to 4+ Mt
Třinecké železárny plans EAF transition by 2030 (awaiting €1.7B government support)
